name: center-container
role: Outer container. Owns the centering mechanism (grid place-items, margin-inline auto, or flex align) based on data-axis. Owns the max-width that defines the readable measure.

axis.note: 'both' uses display:grid;place-items:center;height:100%. 'horizontal' uses margin-inline:auto + max-width. 'vertical' uses flex column with justify-content:center.

max-width.note: narrow=45ch (prose measure). standard=66ch. wide=80ch. none = no max-width.

axisCSSRecipes.horizontal: max-width: var(...); margin-inline: auto; padding-inline: var(...);
axisCSSRecipes.vertical: display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; min-height: 100%;

name: center-content
role: Inner content wrapper. Carries no styling of its own; serves as a stable child reference so the container's centering mechanism has exactly one item to center on (especially in the grid place-items case).

meta.whyTwoSlots: Separating container from content lets the container own positioning (grid/flex/margin) and the content own its own sizing/styling. This also allows the operator to wrap content in a semantic landmark (<article>, <main>) while keeping the centering layer purely structural.
